verses.kjv: And Abram went up out of Egypt, he, and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the south.
verses.ct: So Abram left Egypt, traveling into the Negev with his wife, everything he owned, and Lot accompanying him.
verses.web: Abram went up out of Egypt—he, his wife, all that he had, and Lot with him—into the South.

verses.kjv: And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.
verses.ct: Abram had become extremely wealthy, possessing livestock, silver, and gold.
verses.web: Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

verses.kjv: And he went on his journeys from the south even to Bethel, unto the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Hai;
verses.ct: From the Negev, he continued traveling from place to place until he reached Bethel, the spot where he had originally set up his tent, between Bethel and Hai.
verses.web: He went on his journeys from the South as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,

verses.kjv: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.
verses.ct: This was the location of the altar he had built earlier, and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.
verses.web: to the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first. There Abram called on LORD’s name.

verses.kjv: And Lot also, which went with Abram, had flocks, and herds, and tents.
verses.ct: Lot, who was traveling with Abram, also owned flocks, herds, and tents.
verses.web: Lot also, who went with Abram, had flocks, herds, and tents.

verses.kjv: And the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great, so that they could not dwell together.
verses.ct: The land could not support both of them living in the same area, because their possessions were so extensive that they were unable to remain together.
verses.web: The land was not able to bear them, that they might live together; for their possessions were so great that they couldn’t live together.

verses.kjv: And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram’s cattle and the herdmen of Lot’s cattle: and the Canaanite and the Perizzite dwelled then in the land.
verses.ct: Conflict broke out between those who tended Abram's livestock and those who tended Lot's livestock. At that time, the Canaanite and the Perizzite were also living in the region.
verses.web: There was strife between the herdsmen of Abram’s livestock and the herdsmen of Lot’s livestock. The Canaanites and the Perizzites lived in the land at that time.

verses.kjv: And Abram said unto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee, and between my herdmen and thy herdmen; for we be brethren.
verses.ct: Abram told Lot, "Please, let there be no conflict between you and me, or between your herdsmen and mine, since we are family."
verses.web: Abram said to Lot, “Please, let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herdsmen and my herdsmen; for we are relatives.

verses.kjv: Is not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me: if thou wilt take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if thou depart to the right hand, then I will go to the left.
verses.ct: "The entire land lies open before you. Please, go your own way apart from me. If you choose the left, I will take the right. If you head right, I will go left."
verses.web: Isn’t the whole land before you? Please separate yourself from me. If you go to the left hand, then I will go to the right. Or if you go to the right hand, then I will go to the left.”

verses.kjv: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and beheld all the plain of Jordan, that it was well watered every where, before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, even as the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as thou comest unto Zoar.
verses.ct: Lot looked around and saw that the whole Jordan valley was richly watered everywhere—this was before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah—resembling the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt in the direction of Zoar.
verses.web: Lot lifted up his eyes, and saw all the plain of the Jordan, that it was well-watered everywhere, before LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, like the garden of LORD, like the land of Egypt, as you go to Zoar.

verses.kjv: Then Lot chose him all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.
verses.ct: So Lot selected the entire Jordan valley for himself and headed east. In this way, the two of them parted company.
verses.web: So Lot chose the Plain of the Jordan for himself. Lot traveled east, and they separated themselves from one other.

verses.kjv: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plain, and pitched his tent toward Sodom.
verses.ct: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot made his home among the cities of the valley, setting up his tent near Sodom.
verses.web: Abram lived in the land of Canaan, and Lot lived in the cities of the plain, and moved his tent as far as Sodom.

verses.kjv: But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly.
verses.ct: The people of Sodom, however, were extremely wicked and full of sin against the LORD.
verses.web: Now the men of Sodom were exceedingly wicked and sinners against LORD.

verses.kjv: And the LORD said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward:
verses.ct: After Lot had gone his separate way, the LORD spoke to Abram: "Raise your eyes and look out from where you are standing—to the north, south, east, and west."
verses.web: LORD said to Abram, after Lot was separated from him, “Now, lift up your eyes, and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward,

verses.kjv: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed for ever.
verses.ct: "Every part of the land that you can see, I will give to you and to your descendants forever."
verses.web: for I will give all the land which you see to you and to your offspring forever.

verses.kjv: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, then shall thy seed also be numbered.
verses.ct: "I will make your descendants as numerous as the dust of the earth, so that if anyone could count every particle of dust, only then could your descendants be counted."
verses.web: I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if a man can count the dust of the earth, then your offspring may also be counted.

verses.kjv: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee.
verses.ct: "Get up and walk across the land, covering its full length and width, because I will give it to you."
verses.web: Arise, walk through the land in its length and in its width; for I will give it to you.”

verses.kjv: Then Abram removed his tent, and came and dwelt in the plain of Mamre, which is in Hebron, and built there an altar unto the LORD.
verses.ct: So Abram moved his tent and went to live at the oaks of Mamre in Hebron, where he built an altar to the LORD.
verses.web: Abram moved his tent, and came and lived by the oaks of Mamre, which are in Hebron, and built an altar there to LORD.
